HoloEngine.notes: Core runtime trust layer and target SOC 2 readiness workstream.

HoloGov.notes: Governance, selector, deterministic gates, and final verdict controls.

HoloVerify.notes: Supports evaluation discipline but is not production customer payload handling.

HoloChat.notes: In scope only if deployed as part of a customer-facing production system.

HoloBuild.notes: In scope only if deployed as part of a customer-facing production system.

HoloBrain.notes: In scope only if enabled in a deployment.

soc_2_trust_services_mapping.current_assessment: Target controls identified; several implementation and evidence gaps remain.
Mostly missing / TBD until deployment architecture, SLOs, DR, and monitoring are defined.
Partially supported by target architecture; technical verification and policy evidence needed.
Conditional and mostly TBD pending product terms, data inventory, and deployment-specific flows.
Partially supported by benchmark discipline and HoloGov target design; production evidence needed.

supported_by_existing_architecture_or_be.current_support: HoloEngine, HoloGov, HoloVerify, HoloChat, HoloBuild, and HoloBrain scope boundaries are explicitly separated.
Production customer-mode is separated from HoloVerify benchmark/evidence mode.
Default log design excludes customer payload text, full prompts, raw provider outputs, private source documents, and unredacted conversations.
Production flow calls for bounded payloads and avoidance of unnecessary raw transcript accumulation.
HoloEngine target response is a decision review output, not a downstream business-action execution claim.
Target metadata includes selector version/hash, route, deterministic gate status, and final verdict metadata.

supported_by_existing_architecture_or_be.control_area: Benchmark trace and failure preservation discipline
supported_by_existing_architecture_or_be.current_support: HoloVerify may preserve frozen prompts, raw outputs, scoring maps, traces, runtime manifests, failure autopsies, and operational notes for benchmark integrity.
Do-not-overclaim rules already prohibit unsupported claims about SOC 2 certification, provider retention, TLS, HIPAA, HITRUST, RAM-only processing, and related items.

partially_supported.partial_support: Identity/auth boundary is described as a target production stage.
partially_supported.gap_to_close: Implement authentication mechanism, policies, MFA/service credentials, session/token controls, and evidence.

partially_supported.partial_support: Authorization is included in target request boundary.
partially_supported.gap_to_close: Define roles, permissions, least-privilege model, admin access, service access, and review cadence.

partially_supported.partial_support: Tenant ID/request ID binding is a target control.
partially_supported.gap_to_close: Prove cross-tenant isolation in application, storage, logs, queues, evidence stores, and provider routing.

partially_supported.partial_support: Encryption/TLS verification is identified as a gap.
partially_supported.gap_to_close: Verify deployed TLS configuration, provider API transport, internal transport, and certificate management.

partially_supported.partial_support: Cryptography verification is listed as a target control.
partially_supported.gap_to_close: Verify database, object store, log store, backup, and evidence-store encryption.

partially_supported.partial_support: Provider risk placeholders exist for OpenAI, xAI, and MiniMax.
partially_supported.gap_to_close: Collect DPAs, subprocessor terms, retention settings, zero-retention eligibility, regional handling, and contract evidence.

partially_supported.partial_support: Metadata fields are defined for audit logging.
partially_supported.gap_to_close: Implement centralized logging, alerting, access control, retention, integrity protection, and operational monitoring.

partially_supported.partial_support: Metadata-only default and customer-enabled evidence-retention mode are defined.
partially_supported.gap_to_close: Define retention schedules, deletion workflows, customer request handling, legal hold handling, and evidence-store disposal.

partially_supported.partial_support: Selector logic, worker contracts, runtime manifests, and release changes are named as change-control targets.
partially_supported.gap_to_close: Formalize approvals, testing gates, release records, rollback, model/provider change control, and emergency change process.

partially_supported.partial_support: Secure development is listed as a readiness control area.
partially_supported.gap_to_close: Define code review, dependency scanning, secret scanning, threat modeling, security tests, and release criteria.

partially_supported.partial_support: Benchmark mode preserves traces and manifests.
partially_supported.gap_to_close: Add production trace integrity controls, hashes, tamper-evidence, access controls, and retention policy.
Define production incident and model-failure autopsy process, owner, timelines, evidence capture, and corrective actions.

missing_or_tbd.current_gap: Need documented security incident plan, severity model, escalation paths, customer notification process, evidence preservation, and post-incident review.
Need backup policy, restore tests, RTO/RPO, DR runbook, dependency mapping, and recovery evidence.
Need scanning process, severity SLAs, patching workflow, penetration test plan, dependency inventory, and exception process.
Need support access approval, just-in-time access, logging, customer authorization, break-glass controls, and support-data minimization.
Need maintained risk register with owner, likelihood, impact, treatment, residual risk, and review cadence.
Need onboarding/offboarding, acceptable use, background check policy if applicable, access reviews, and security training evidence.
Need current inventory of services, databases, evidence stores, model routes, providers, logs, secrets, repositories, and production environments.
Need data inventory, privacy notice, DPA/customer terms, deletion request handling, purpose limitation, and subprocessor disclosures if personal data is processed.

control_gaps.current_state: Target boundary exists, implementation evidence not collected.
control_gaps.gap_closure_work: Define authentication architecture, MFA/service auth, token/session controls, account lifecycle, and test evidence.
Define role matrix, least privilege, admin roles, support roles, service accounts, approval workflow, and access review evidence.
Prove isolation across runtime, storage, logs, traces, evidence-retention mode, queues, and provider routing.

control_gaps.current_state: TLS must be verified in deployment.
control_gaps.gap_closure_work: Document TLS configuration, certificate management, provider API transport, internal service transport, and verification evidence.
Verify encryption for databases, log stores, evidence stores, backups, local artifacts, and key management.
Centralize secrets storage, rotation, access logging, CI/CD secret handling, and secret scanning.

control_gaps.current_state: Audit-log field design exists; operational monitoring not evidenced.
control_gaps.gap_closure_work: Implement centralized logs, alerts, SIEM or equivalent, anomaly detection, uptime checks, error budgets, and log access controls.
Create IR plan, severity levels, communications plan, customer notification rules, evidence preservation, table-top exercises, and corrective-action tracking.
Build provider/subprocessor inventory, DPAs, retention settings, zero-retention verification where available, vendor reviews, and annual review process.

control_gaps.current_state: Default metadata-only and customer-enabled evidence-retention mode are target controls.
control_gaps.gap_closure_work: Define retention schedule, deletion workflow, customer evidence-retention settings, legal holds, backups deletion model, and disposal evidence.

control_gaps.current_state: Versioning targets exist for selectors, worker contracts, manifests, and releases.
control_gaps.gap_closure_work: Implement release approvals, model/provider change reviews, test evidence, rollback records, emergency change process, and production deployment logs.

control_gaps.current_state: Not yet evidenced as an operating program.
control_gaps.gap_closure_work: Add code review policy, branch protection, SAST/dependency scanning, secret scanning, secure design review, security test gate, and release checklist.
Define backup scope, restore testing, RTO/RPO, DR runbook, dependency failure response, and periodic validation.
Define vulnerability intake, scanning cadence, severity SLAs, patch management, pen test cadence, and exception process.
Define support access workflow, customer approval, just-in-time elevation, logging, redaction, break-glass review, and support data minimization.

holoengine_specific_ai_controls.current_support: Architecture separates production customer-mode from HoloVerify benchmark/evidence mode.
holoengine_specific_ai_controls.gap_or_required_evidence: Prove storage, access, retention, logging, and claim-language separation in deployed environments.

holoengine_specific_ai_controls.current_support: Target audit fields include route and model/provider versions.
holoengine_specific_ai_controls.gap_or_required_evidence: Capture provider, model, route, prompt-minimization flag, latency, token counts, retry/error status, and retention mode without default payload logging.

holoengine_specific_ai_controls.current_support: Target HoloGov metadata includes selector version/hash.
holoengine_specific_ai_controls.gap_or_required_evidence: Produce runtime logs and release artifacts showing selector hash/version for each decision.

holoengine_specific_ai_controls.current_support: Benchmark/evidence mode recognizes scoring maps as preserved artifacts separate from production.
holoengine_specific_ai_controls.gap_or_required_evidence: Access-control scoring maps, exclude scoring maps from provider prompts, prove production runtime cannot read benchmark scoring maps, and audit leakage tests.

holoengine_specific_ai_controls.current_support: Benchmark traces and runtime manifests are preserved for reproducibility.
holoengine_specific_ai_controls.gap_or_required_evidence: Add hash manifests, immutable/tamper-evident trace storage, access logs, and chain-of-custody for production evidence-retention mode.

holoengine_specific_ai_controls.current_support: Benchmark mode includes failure autopsies and operational notes.
holoengine_specific_ai_controls.gap_or_required_evidence: Define production failure taxonomy, owner, autopsy template, corrective-action workflow, customer impact review, and regression coverage.

holoengine_specific_ai_controls.current_support: Provider/model versions are target audit metadata.
holoengine_specific_ai_controls.gap_or_required_evidence: Formalize model onboarding, deprecation, route changes, eval gates, rollback, risk signoff, and customer notice criteria.

holoengine_specific_ai_controls.current_support: Architecture defines explicit customer-enabled evidence-retention mode.
holoengine_specific_ai_controls.gap_or_required_evidence: Implement customer configuration, retention period, access controls, encryption, deletion, export, audit logging, and support-access boundaries.
Prove minimization with request schemas, prompt builders, redaction tests, and logs showing payload text is excluded by default.

holoengine_specific_ai_controls.current_support: HoloEngine returns ALLOW/ESCALATE and does not claim downstream execution by default.
holoengine_specific_ai_controls.gap_or_required_evidence: Define customer responsibility for escalations, operator workflow, support procedures, and product UI/API wording.

Final system description, architecture diagram, data-flow diagram, in-scope services, out-of-scope surfaces.
Auth design, MFA/service credential policy, token/session settings, account lifecycle evidence, test results.
Role matrix, permission model, admin/support access approvals, quarterly access reviews.
Tenant model, isolation tests, storage/log/evidence partitioning proof, cross-tenant negative tests.
Secrets inventory, storage mechanism, rotation policy, access logs, secret scanning results.
Release policy, approvals, test gates, deployment records, rollback procedure, emergency change records.
Code review policy, branch protection, SAST/dependency scanning, threat model, secure design review records.
Scan reports, dependency inventory, vulnerability SLAs, remediation tickets, exception register, pen test plan.
Audit-log schema, centralized logging evidence, alert rules, access controls, log retention settings, sample logs.
IR policy, severity matrix, on-call/escalation process, customer notification template, tabletop evidence.
Provider list, subprocessors, DPAs, retention terms, vendor reviews, annual review calendar.
TLS configuration, certificate management, provider API transport verification, internal transport verification.
Database, log store, object store, backup, and evidence-store encryption proof; key management evidence.
Request schemas, prompt builder controls, redaction tests, default excluded logging fields, sample metadata-only logs.
Retention schedule, deletion SOP, customer request workflow, backup deletion model, evidence-retention mode policy.
Customer configuration, retention controls, access control, deletion/export workflow, audit logging, support restrictions.
Uptime checks, alert thresholds, on-call runbook, dependency monitoring, incident records.
Backup policy, backup inventory, restore test evidence, RTO/RPO, backup access controls.
DR plan, dependency map, failover runbook, recovery test, post-test corrective actions.
Schema validation tests, malformed request rejection tests, action-boundary checks, tenant routing tests.
Runtime audit samples with selector version/hash, release manifest, selector change approval record.
Route logs with provider/model version, route ID, retry/error status, latency, token counts, retention flag.
Benchmark scoring-map access controls, prompt-leakage tests, production runtime separation proof, negative tests.
Trace hash manifest, immutable/tamper-evident storage configuration, access logs, chain-of-custody procedure.
Autopsy template, failure taxonomy, corrective-action tickets, regression test links, customer impact review.
Model route inventory, evaluation gate, approval records, rollback plan, deprecation process, customer notice criteria.
Customer-facing data handling terms, purpose limitation, DPA terms, privacy notice, product data map.
Request intake, identity verification, deletion workflow, SLA, evidence retention exceptions, completion evidence.
Support access approval, just-in-time access, customer authorization, access logs, redaction, break-glass review.

safe_to_say_now: HoloEngine has a draft data-flow architecture for enterprise trust review.
The architecture separates target production customer-mode handling from benchmark/evidence mode.
The target production design is metadata-only audit logging by default.
Customer-enabled evidence-retention mode is a planned/target control, not a default payload-retention claim.
HoloEngine's target output boundary is ALLOW or ESCALATE; it should not be described as executing downstream business actions by default.
HoloGov target metadata includes model/provider route, selector version/hash, verdict, latency, token counts, error status, retry status, and evidence-retention mode flag.
Provider/vendor handling is explicitly listed as a verification workstream, including DPAs, subprocessor review, retention settings, and zero-retention eligibility where applicable.
HoloVerify benchmark evidence is separate from production customer payload handling.
HoloEngine has a defined list of trust controls and gaps to close before a formal SOC 2 audit.
must_remain_tbd: Whether any specific deployment is ready for SOC 2 audit.
The final in-scope system boundary for HoloChat, HoloBuild, or HoloBrain in a customer deployment.
Encryption in transit and at rest verification for the deployed environment.
Provider retention settings, DPA status, subprocessor terms, data residency, and zero-retention eligibility.
Production logging, monitoring, SIEM, and incident response operating evidence.
Backup, restore, and disaster recovery evidence.
Privacy notices, deletion workflow, retention schedule, and support-access procedures.
Model change control process and production trace integrity evidence.
must_not_be_claimed: Do not claim SOC 2 compliance, certification, auditor validation, Type I completion, or Type II completion.
Do not claim HoloEngine is SOC 2 ready.
Do not claim controls are implemented or operating effectively unless supported by current evidence.
Do not claim provider zero retention without provider-specific contract proof.
Do not claim TLS 1.3, encryption coverage, or key-management specifics without deployment verification.
Do not claim production customer data is never stored unless the deployed system, logs, provider flows, backups, and evidence modes prove that statement.
Do not claim benchmark evidence handling is the same as production customer data handling.
Do not claim HoloEngine executes downstream customer business actions unless a separate deployment explicitly implements and controls that execution layer.

recommended_roadmap.name: trust binder
recommended_roadmap.goal: Create the evidence binder before making stronger buyer-facing statements.
deliverables: production versus benchmark/evidence mode boundary memo
claim-safety FAQ for sales and buyer review
exit_criteria: every control in the binder has an owner, status, evidence location, and next action
all buyer-facing claims are labeled implemented, target, verify, or TBD
no SOC 2 compliance, certification, readiness, or auditor-validation claims are present

recommended_roadmap.name: SOC 2 readiness/gap closure
recommended_roadmap.goal: Close high-priority design and evidence gaps before auditor scoping.
deliverables: AI governance controls for model routing, selector hashes, no scoring-map leakage, trace integrity, failure autopsies, and model change control
exit_criteria: high-priority missing controls are implemented or explicitly risk-accepted
evidence samples exist for each implemented control
auditor can review scope without relying on unsupported claims

recommended_roadmap.name: SOC 2 Type I
recommended_roadmap.goal: Have an independent auditor assess control design at a point in time.
prerequisites: evidence binder complete enough for design assessment
management assertion drafted by qualified counsel/audit advisors
recommended_roadmap.allowed_claim_after_completion_only_if_t: HoloEngine completed a SOC 2 Type I examination for the specified scope and report period.
recommended_roadmap.claim_boundary: Do not claim Type II operating effectiveness from Type I work.

recommended_roadmap.name: SOC 2 Type II
recommended_roadmap.goal: Have an independent auditor assess control design and operating effectiveness over an observation period.
prerequisites: controls operating for the defined period
recommended_roadmap.allowed_claim_after_completion_only_if_t: HoloEngine completed a SOC 2 Type II examination for the specified scope and observation period.
recommended_roadmap.claim_boundary: Do not claim broader certification, compliance, or control coverage outside the report scope.

immediate_next_actions: Assign owners to each evidence binder control.
Create an evidence folder/index for Trust, Security, Privacy, Engineering, SRE, Legal, Support, HoloGov, and HoloVerify artifacts.
Convert all partial, missing, and verify items into tracked tickets.
Produce a buyer-safe security FAQ that uses only target/TBD language where proof is not yet collected.
Schedule an auditor scoping call after Phase 0 binder artifacts exist.
